title: Chief Financial Officer
description:
Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Finance & Leadership

  • Act as a key strategic partner to the CEO on financial performance, forecasting, risk management and strategic decision-making to drive measurable business growth
  • Provide financial insights on capital allocation, ROI analysis, project and vessel profitability, and evolving maritime business models
  • Drive alignment of financial and operational resources to maximize project outcomes and overall business value

Financial Operations, Planning & Reporting

  • Lead financial operations to ensure accurate accounting, effective cost control and sound corporate finance management aligned with business strategy
  • Oversee financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis to support data-driven decision-making
  • Prepare and present monthly and quarterly financial reports and cash-flow projections to the CEO and Board
  • Oversee the preparation of consolidated financial statements, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements
  • Drive continuous improvement in financial processes, systems, controls and data quality to support scalability and growth

Governance, Risk & Compliance

  • Develop and implement governance frameworks to strengthen internal controls, compliance, and risk management within the company
  • Ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards, statutory, tax, and regulatory requirements
  • Coordinate with external auditors, legal counsel, and tax advisors on audits, compliance, structuring, and cross-border transactions

Capital Management & Fundraising

  • Manage banking relationships, financing facilities and capital market activities
  • Lead capital-raising initiatives including equity, debt, grants and strategic financing relevant to maritime and infrastructure projects
  • Partner with the CEO, investors, banks and advisors on financial modelling, due diligence, and investor materials
  • Lead and support fundraising activities by preparing financial models, pitch materials, data rooms and responses to investor due diligence

Investor & Stakeholder Relations

  • Manage investor relations and communications with the Board of Directors and external stakeholders
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with banks, financial institutions, investment funds, auditors and advisors
  • Ensure transparency, accuracy and credibility in all financial communications


Qualifications and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or Economics preferred. Open to candidates with Bachelor’s degrees in other disciplines
  • Professional qualification (CPA, ACCA, CFA) or equivalent are strongly preferred
  • 10+ years of finance experience, with at least 5 years in senior finance leadership
  • Experience in maritime and shipping preferred
  • Proven track record of driving financial performance and business growth with strong financial modelling, analysis and reporting skills.
  • Proficient in both English and Chinese language



Competencies:

  • Strong strategic thinking and business acumen, with the ability to advise on capital allocation, ROI and project profitability
  • Proven leadership and ability to build, mentor and oversee finance team
  • Hands-on experience in financial planning, analysis, reporting and governance frameworks
  • Ability to liaise effectively with the CEO, Board, investors, banks, auditors and government agencies
  • Expertise in fundraising, including preparing financial models, investor presentations, data rooms and supporting due diligence
  • Familiarity with financial and ERP systems platforms to drive efficiency and data accuracy
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
  • Strong understanding of Singapore financial regulations and compliance requirements

postedCompany.description:
Maritime is the backbone of commerce transporting 90% of the world’s trade.
But there is a critical Maritime crisis - vessels are heavily reliant on diesel. If
nothing radical is done to shape up the industry, it is projected to be
responsible for 17% of the world’s total annual CO2 emissions by 2050.
Specifically, the coastal maritime sector is technologically lagging, and its
ultra-fragmented nature results in a lack of collective synergy to push for
transformation. Coastal vessels are at high risk of becoming irrelevant in the
new green economy.

The time is now for us to make a difference.

Introducing Pyxis, a fleet of first-of-its-kind electrified vessels which are
energy-efficient, maintenance-lite, and digitalised. The electric vessels will be
leased or sold to existing vessel owners, providing a significant leap in vessel
performance, changing the way services are provided to the maritime sector.
Pyxis will revolutionise the coastal maritime industry and sail us towards a
carbon-free and sustainable future.

Pyxis is led by maritime veterans and supported by leading Singapore coastal
vessel owners and industry partners. We will target key port services segments
in Singapore and scale up to sister-ports in the region.

With the sustainability and decarbonisation movements disrupting the maritime
industry, Pyxis will allow for ports around the world to operate greener, cleaner,
and more efficiently.

There is no better time than now, and no better place than from Maritime’s
global hub, Singapore, to begin this maritime transformation.

Will you join us and be part of this journey towards a decarbonised future?
postedCompany.name: PYXIS MARITIME PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D05 Clementi New Town, Hong Leong Garden, Pasir Panjang
